notes: Reused from cache. Fiscal 1982 results being restated downwards; booked shipments did not result in immediate revenues. Stock price $24.125 at publication.
Compensation plan until recently skewed heavily toward bookings; resulted in bookings that did not convert to shipments. Stock price $18 at publication.
Early compensation scheme skewed to customer payment; turned salesmen into 'dunning agents'; ensured initial months of quarter spent collecting rather than selling. Stock price $40 at publication.

successor: Atari Inc. sold to Jack Tramiel (1984); Warner merged with Time Inc. to form Time Warner (1990)
notes: Cited as example of annual-cycle hockey stick risk: 'last few weeks of the final quarter largely determine the year.' Atari's debacle cited as investment-magnitude example of an annual hockey stick.
Author; co-founder Aberdeen Group. Address: Wayland, Massachusetts. Notes 'from personal experience' regarding the backlog trap.
